description: Viral video of sea turtle with straw in its nose (2015) catalyzed a global anti-plastic-straw movement peaking in 2018. Support for bans grew rapidly. Starbucks, cities, and corporations adopted bans. Tests viral-media-driven opinion shift and environmental norm cascading.

key_events.description: Viral sea turtle video resurfaces on social media, reaching 70M+ views. Emotional content drives rapid awareness and support.
Seattle becomes first major US city to ban plastic straws (July 2018). Policy legitimizes the movement.
Starbucks announces eliminating plastic straws from all stores globally by 2020. Corporate norm cascade begins.
EU Parliament votes to ban single-use plastics including straws. International policy momentum peaks.
Disability rights advocates push back, highlighting need for flexible straws. Minor counter-narrative emerges but doesn't reverse trend.

calibration_notes: Tests viral-media-driven norm cascade. Key patterns: single viral emotional content piece as catalyst, low initial salience but high persuadability (large undecided), corporate bandwagon effect accelerating support, policy adoption legitimizing position, counter-arguments (disability access, environmental impact negligible) had limited effect on overall trajectory. Ipsos/YouGov polling data used.
